_doc:
Phase 13 — release showcase. The HDL toolchain has reached v2.0: bidirectional Verilog (export + import) with a property-based fuzz suite (1000 seeds, 1000 pass) backing the round-trip claim.

Representative corpus under `examples/hdl-corpus/`:
  • phase8-mux2-handwritten.v   — 2:1 MUX, parameters, attributes, async-reset always, $display.
  • phase12-fidelity-demo.v     — small ALU exercising Fidelity Mode round-trip.
  • bcd-counter.v               — 4-bit BCD counter with rollover (Phase-3 + Phase-4 cross).
  • fsm-traffic-light.v         — 3-state Moore FSM (case → MUX inference).
  • small-alu.v                 — 4-bit ALU, mixed arithmetic + bitwise + comparator.
  • uart-tx.v                   — 8-N-1 UART transmitter, 4-state FSM with bit-counter.

Each file imports cleanly, exports back, and the corpus round-trip suite (`node examples/tests/test-hdl-corpus-round-trip.mjs`) confirms FIDELITY mode preserves the original module header verbatim.

Phase 13 verified via:
  • run-hdl.mjs                       — 25/25 demos green.
  • test-hdl-fuzz.mjs                 — 1000/1000 fuzz seeds pass.
  • test-hdl-corpus-round-trip.mjs    — 6 corpus files × CANONICAL + FIDELITY = 12/12.
  • test-hdl-toCircuit-l3-gate.mjs    — 25/25 demos survive export → import → re-export.
  • test-hdl-elaborate-l1-gate.mjs    — 25/25 demos exact through full pipeline.
  • Plus 250+ unit checks across parser / lexer / elaborate / inference / layout / import-UX / fidelity / lower-tristate.

See [SUPPORTED.md](js/hdl/SUPPORTED.md) for the full capability matrix and known limitations. [INSTALL.md](INSTALL.md) covers the optional iverilog / Yosys / nextpnr dependencies. [README.md](README.md) → "HDL Quickstart" has the user-facing flow.
